description: GS1900-8HP B1 (formerly v1) 8-port PoE+ managed gigabit switch, Realtek RTL8380M, 70W PoE budget

hardware.serial: UART header, 115200 8N1, 3.3V (left side of PCB, labeled VCC/TX/RX/GND)
hardware.note: Dual-partition OEM device. Shares PCB with non-PoE GS1900-8. PoE MCU initialized by bootloader at power-on (all ports PoE+ enabled by default). MCU state lost on reboot — PoE devices briefly lose power during restart. CRITICAL: Serial numbers starting S21+ must run stock v2.70+ first or PoE can be irreversibly bricked.

oem-http.description: Upload initramfs image via OEM web UI HTTP upload endpoint using curl. Two-stage: OEM upload boots initramfs, then sysupgrade writes permanently. OEM web UI uses frameset-based navigation with dispatcher.cgi cmd numbers. Same ZyXEL firmware stack as GS1900-24E.

sysupgrade.description: SSH sysupgrade from existing OpenWrt (initramfs or permanent OpenWrt). Works for same-version or compatible version upgrades.

mtd-write.description: Direct MTD partition write from running OpenWrt initramfs. Used when sysupgrade rejects images due to partition layout changes between versions.

serial-tftp-openwrt.description: Flash via serial + TFTP. Requires FTDI adapter (3.3V) connected to UART header on left side of PCB. Two-stage: boot initramfs via TFTP, then sysupgrade. Interrupt U-Boot with space bar within 1 second.
serial-tftp-openwrt.reset_instructions: Connect FTDI serial adapter to UART header (3.3V, left side PCB). Interrupt U-Boot with space bar within 1 second at boot.

uboot_env.note: bootdelay=1 set by OEM. Dual-partition device — use 'setsys bootpartition 0' and 'savesys' before TFTP to ensure flashing primary partition.

partition_layout.note: Dual-partition layout (firmware + runtime2). Layout may change to merged single-partition after OpenWrt 25.12.1 sysupgrade (same as GS1900-24E behavior).

recovery.note: Dual-partition device. Use 'setsys bootpartition 0' + 'savesys' to select primary partition before TFTP flash.

recovery_commands.note: Return to stock: switch to backup partition (has OEM firmware). From OEM web UI, flash primary partition with stock firmware.

oem_login.source: Inferred from GS1900-24E (same ZyXEL firmware stack)

oem_firmware_page.source: Inferred from GS1900-24E (same ZyXEL firmware stack)
oem_firmware_page.confirm_dialog: Do you really want to reboot?

uboot_banner.example: U-Boot Version: 2.0.0.60266 (Jul 17 2015 - 12:29:50) CPU: 500MHz DRAM: 128 MB FLASH: 16 MB Model: ZyXEL_GS1900_8HP

openwrt_ready.note: No LuCI web UI in default build (switch device, no wifi). SSH only.

default_config.source: openwrt.org wiki - Power over Ethernet section

port_config_template.note: PoE port IDs match interface numbers: lan1=1, lan2=2, ..., lan8=8

poe.note: Bootloader initializes PoE MCU at power-on, enabling PoE+ on all ports. MCU state not preserved across reboots — PoE devices briefly lose power during switch restart. realtek-poe package included in OpenWrt images for this device. Default config only manages port 1; must add config sections for ports 2-8.

safety.serial_number_warning: Devices with serial numbers starting S21 or higher MUST run stock firmware v2.70+ before any firmware changes. Older stock firmware can irreversibly brick PoE capabilities regardless of subsequent firmware.
safety.dual_partition_note: Dual-partition device. Keep OEM firmware on backup partition (partition 1) for recovery. Use 'setsys bootpartition 0' to ensure OpenWrt boots from primary.
safety.poe_power_loss: All PoE devices will briefly lose power during switch reboot (MCU reinitializes). Plan accordingly for sensitive equipment.
